concerns.text: FR-007 mandates: 'the GNN MUST use attention mechanisms to handle collinearity'. The plan.md 'Model Architecture' section states: 'The GNN uses attention mechanisms (GAT layers or attention pooling)...' but the 'Technical Context' and 'Project Structure' list `gcn.py` (Graph Convolutional Network) as the primary model. Standard GCNs do not inherently use attention mechanisms (unlike GATs). The plan does not explicitly confirm the implementation of GAT or attention pooling layers, creating a gap between the mandatory FR-007 requirement and the described model architecture.

FR-008 mandates: 'System MUST apply Bonferroni or Holm correction to p-values if the count of p-values in results.csv > 1'. The plan.md 'Statistical Methodology' section mentions: 'If multiple tests are run... apply Bonferroni or Holm correction'. However, the 'Project Structure' lists `perm_test.py` which typically generates a single p-value for the main hypothesis. The plan does not explicitly describe a scenario where multiple p-values are generated requiring correction, nor does it detail the logic for the 'count > 1' condition. This risks the implementation missing the conditional correction logic required by FR-008.

SC-005 requires measuring 'Multiple-comparison correction... if the count of p-values in results.csv > 1'. The plan.md 'Statistical Methodology' discusses the correction but does not explicitly state how the `results.csv` (or `stats.csv` as per Project Structure) will be structured to support this check (i.e., will it contain multiple p-values?). The plan lacks a clear data-model definition for the output file that enables the verification of SC-005.
